markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Extract TypeScript/TSX modules, components, hooks, functions, types, imports, tests, and JSX element IDs. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: TypeScriptSymbolExtractor leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: TypeScriptSymbolExtractor is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commits 5d6490b0 (compiling foundation) + 79e1a7e4 (runtime proof); 43 lib + 11 integration + 1 nav-API tests green on real PostgreSQL under runtime-full,test-utils. Found+fixed impl-Type/struct-Type entity collision. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: TS overload signatures collide: `function f(a:number):void; function f(a:string):void { }` produces multiple function_declaration nodes all named `f` -> same symbol_path `f` -> same entity_key -> merge in upsert (knowledge.rs:3336). Same for overloaded method_definition inside a class (`Service.run` x2). The delegate only proved the struct/impl Rust collision; the TS overload shape is unproven and silently merges.

Same-name across declaration kinds: TS legally allows `interface Foo {}` and `function Foo(){}` AND `class Foo{}` AND `namespace Foo{}` to coexist (declaration merging). All map to symbol_path `Foo`, same entity_kind=symbol, same entity_key -> merge. The index cannot represent a merged interface+namespace; one overwrites the other.

ts_const_binding only inspects the FIRST function-valued declarator (symbols.rs:389 'return Some' on first hit); `const a = () => {}, b = () => {}` (multiple declarators in one lexical_declaration) drops `b`. Under-extraction, not a crash.

Component/Hook heuristic is name-shape only (is_component_name = first char uppercase, symbols.rs:370): a PascalCase const that is NOT a React component (e.g. `const Config = () => ({...})` a factory) is mislabeled Component. Provenance noise, low severity.
evasion_probes: Read extract_typescript (symbols.rs:298-347): each declaration node independently emits a symbol keyed on decl_name; no dedup/merge-awareness, so two same-named decls collide. TS scope kinds (class_declaration, internal_module, module) DO scope methods under their class so Service.run is distinct from a free run - that part is sound.
Confirmed .d.ts routed to TypeScript grammar (parser.rs:94) and TSX is a distinct grammar (Tsx) so JSX-bearing .tsx parses; .ts with JSX would error but that is user error.
Verified empty-name guard (symbols.rs:330) skips anonymous decls.
evidence_checked: delegate test typescript_extracts_functions_classes_interfaces_components_hooks (single decl per name only)

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG/unit test green (knowledge_code_index_tests 13 + nav_api 2 + 59 unit, 0 fail).

evidence: Hardened b0266f35: TS declaration-merging (interface/function/class/namespace) + overloads disambiguated by kind; all const declarators extracted.

findings.title: Same positional-ordinal churn as MT-098 applies to TS overloads / declaration-merging discriminated by kind tag + ordinal
findings.evidence: TS symbols carry ts_js_kind_tag disambiguators (symbols.rs:460,601); same-(path,kind) duplicates (overloads, merged declarations) fall through to assign_collision_ordinals positional dup ordinals.
findings.fix: Same as MT-098: make the discriminator edit-stable (signature/content hash) rather than extraction-order positional.

adversarial_review_v2.note: VERIFIED FIXED from V1 (b0266f35): TS interface/function/class/namespace declaration-merging + overloads no longer collapse (kind-tag disambiguator + ordinal). Residual: positional-ordinal churn.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-099 requires a TypeScript/TSX symbol extractor (functions, components, hooks, classes, interfaces, types, methods, tests) that stores spans/entity-keys in PostgreSQL via Handshake-managed tree-sitter parsers. The implementation does exactly this, the prior V1 adversarial collision findings are genuinely fixed, and the disambiguated keys round-trip through real managed PostgreSQL.
findings: extract_typescript (src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_code_index/symbols.rs:408-480) is a real tree-sitter traversal: emits Function/Component/Hook (ts_function_kind, :502), Class/Interface/TypeAlias/Enum, method_definition, const/arrow bindings, and test-runner calls. No placeholders/escapes anywhere in the TS path (grep for todo!/unimplemented!/deferred/pending MT clean).

V1 adversarial finding #1 (TS declaration-merging interface/class/function Foo collapse) is fixed: every top-level decl carries a kind-tag disambiguator via ts_js_kind_tag (symbols.rs:485) appended to entity_key as ~{tag} (symbols.rs:116-132). Proven by unit test typescript_declaration_merging_same_name_do_not_collide (symbols.rs:1064) asserting unique keys Foo~interface/Foo~class/Foo~component.

V1 finding #3 (ts_const_binding only took first declarator) is fixed: ts_const_bindings (symbols.rs:543-574) iterates ALL variable_declarator children; `const a=()=>{}, b=()=>{}` yields both, non-function consts become Constant. Proven by typescript_multi_declarator_const_extracts_all (symbols.rs:1086).

Residual overload/duplicate collisions handled by assign_collision_ordinals (symbols.rs:140-165): same (path,disambiguator) groups get stable document-ordered dupN suffix so the PG upsert ON CONFLICT(entity_key) never silently merges distinct symbols. Proven by duplicate_same_kind_declarations_get_ordinal (symbols.rs:1107).

Sub-rule 2 (real PG touch): mt112_indexes_mixed_tree_with_symbols_spans_edges (tests/knowledge_code_index_tests.rs:311) indexes app/service.ts + app/widget.tsx through eng.index_code_source then reads symbols back via list_knowledge_entities_by_kind and asserts disambiguated TS keys typescript:app/service.ts#Service~class (:394) and #useThing~hook (:399) and tsx:app/widget.tsx#Widget~component (:406). knowledge_pg() (tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs:108) connects a real PostgreSQL cluster, creates an isolated schema, and runs the full migration chain on PostgresDatabase — not SQLite/in-memory; skips loudly when PG binaries absent. MT evidence records 13 integration + 2 nav tests green on real managed PostgreSQL.

Name-lookup correctness preserved: symbol_simple_name in src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/knowledge_code_nav.rs strips the ~discriminator before parsing the simple name, so the collision tag does not break navigation by name.
Residual adversarial_review_v2 LOW finding (positional-ordinal churn across edits on invalid duplicate-decl code) is a stability nit on an edge case, not a failure of required behavior; non-blocking.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): packet cites v02.192 anchors while current is v02.194; same section numbers resolve. lifecycle.completed_by is null and claimed_by==KERNEL_BUILDER (self-cert not triggered since not signed COMPLETED).
